name: Yaw Bearing
description: A massive slewing ring bearing connecting the nacelle to the tower, allowing the housing to rotate so the blades always face the wind.
whyMatters: Materials — keeps a large precision-machined alloy-steel ring in use instead of reducing it to low-value scrap or disposal.

name: Yaw Control Gear Motor
description: A powerful motor and heavy-duty gearbox that engages the Yaw Bearing to rotate the turbine's entire head, keeping it facing into the wind.
whyMatters: Energy — preserves the machining, heat-treatment, winding and assembly effort already invested in a complex electromechanical unit.

name: Yaw Controller
description: The electronic brain" that controls the yaw system. It takes information from wind sensors and tells the yaw motor when and how much to turn to keep the turbine facing the wind."
whyMatters: Waste avoided — prevents a functional electronics module from becoming e-waste and keeps small but valuable copper and precious-metal content recoverable.

name: Accumulators (small)
description: A compact device that stores hydraulic fluid under pressure. It acts like a hydraulic battery and shock absorber for smaller systems, providing a burst of power when needed and smoothing out pressure spikes.
whyMatters: Materials — keeps a certified pressure shell, fittings and elastomer system in service where safe requalification is possible.

name: Automatic Lubrication Pump with reservoir
description: A self-contained electric pump and grease/oil container that automatically lubricates a machine's moving parts at set intervals, reducing the need for manual maintenance.
whyMatters: Energy — protects a fully assembled service system whose value is in manufacturing, motor winding, pump tolerances and fluid handling hardware.

name: Axial Fan
description: A large, propeller-like fan that moves air straight through the turbine's housing to cool down the generator and other critical components inside the nacelle.
whyMatters: Energy — preserves a complete cooling device rather than scrapping separate metal, motor and polymer parts.

name: Blade (pitch) Bearing
description: A large precision bearing that allows an entire wind turbine blade to rotate along its own axis. This pitching" motion is used to control the turbine's speed and power generation."
whyMatters: Materials — keeps a very large precision bearing and high-grade steel mass in use.

name: Bladder Accumulator
description: A high-capacity hydraulic energy storage unit using a flexible bladder. It's like a high-pressure cushion that smooths out surges and ensures a steady supply of fluid power to large systems.
whyMatters: Materials — preserves a pressure-rated vessel and avoids replacing high-pressure steel and elastomer hardware.

name: Capacitors Epocs
description: Large, heavy-duty electronic components that store and release electrical energy quickly. They improve power quality in the turbine's electrical system and provide surge protection.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — keeps aluminium, polymer film and terminal metals from entering mixed e-waste and protects the manufacturing effort in wound film capacitors.

name: Capacitor-03
description: A mid-sized capacitor used in power electronics to stabilize electrical signals and store small amounts of energy. Often found in motor drives and power circuits.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — prevents a small but material-rich electrical component from becoming mixed e-waste.

name: Capacitor-41
description: A variant of power capacitor used in control systems and power circuits for filtering and energy storage.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — keeps useful aluminium and engineered dielectric material from becoming low-value disposal burden.

name: Centrifugal Blower
description: A fan that spins air outward from its center, creating strong airflow for cooling or ventilation in tight spaces within the turbine system.
whyMatters: Energy — preserves a complete air-moving machine and the embedded manufacturing in the motor, impeller balancing and housing.

name: Choke
description: An electrical component that smooths out electrical current fluctuations. It protects sensitive electronics by filtering out electrical noise and spikes.
whyMatters: Mining pressure — reduces demand for new copper conductors and magnetic core materials.

name: Circuit breaker
description: An automatic safety switch that cuts off electrical power if it detects dangerous conditions like overload or short circuits, protecting the turbine's electrical system.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — tested reuse or controlled recovery prevents copper contacts, metals and thermoset plastics from becoming mixed electrical waste.

name: Composite Turbine Blade
description: A section from a wind turbine blade. It's an advanced composite structure, shaped for perfect aerodynamics, and is both incredibly strong and surprisingly lightweight.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — composite blades are durable but difficult to recycle cost-effectively, so reuse prevents a large engineered composite from becoming landfill or low-value downcycling.

name: Construction Light
description: A rugged, industrial-grade work light designed to withstand harsh conditions on job sites or in industrial environments.
whyMatters: Public value — can become workshop, emergency, school, construction or community lighting while avoiding premature e-waste.

name: Cooling Station
description: A complete cooling system unit that regulates temperature for critical turbine components, typically using liquid coolant circulation.
whyMatters: Energy — preserves a complex thermal-management system with high manufacturing value in pumps, motors and heat-transfer surfaces.

name: CT modules (controllers)
description: Electronic control modules that manage various turbine subsystems, processing sensor data and sending commands to motors and actuators.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — keeps industrial electronics and recoverable conductor metals out of mixed e-waste.

name: Cup Anemometer
description: A wind speed measuring device with spinning cups. The turbine uses its data to optimize blade pitch and yaw direction for maximum energy capture.
whyMatters: Public value — can be reused for education, local weather monitoring, farms, maker labs or community wind mapping.

name: Current Transformer
description: A sensor that measures the amount of electrical current flowing through a circuit without making direct contact, enabling safe monitoring of high-power systems.
whyMatters: Mining pressure — keeps copper and electrical/magnetic steel in service, both important materials for electrification and grid equipment.

name: Electrical Motor
description: A motor that converts electrical energy into mechanical motion, used throughout the turbine for various functions like pumps, fans, and actuators.
whyMatters: Materials — motors are concentrated stores of recyclable copper, steel and iron, and repair/reuse retains much more value than shredding.

name: Ethernet Rail Switch
description: A network switch designed for industrial environments, mounted on a DIN rail. It connects various control systems and sensors for data communication.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — avoids e-waste from a high-function industrial electronics module and supports reuse in controls, labs and training rigs.

name: Filter
description: A device that removes contaminants from hydraulic fluid, air, or other fluids to protect sensitive components and maintain system efficiency.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — reusable housings and proper recovery keep contaminated filter media and metal canisters from becoming unmanaged oily waste.

name: Flexible Busbar
description: A thick, flexible conductor that carries large electrical currents between components while accommodating movement and thermal expansion.
whyMatters: Mining pressure — keeps high-conductivity copper or aluminium in use, reducing pressure for virgin conductor metals.

name: Fuse
description: A sacrificial safety device that protects electrical circuits by breaking the connection when current exceeds safe levels.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — even small sacrificial components contain ceramic, silica and conductive metals that should be handled intentionally rather than mixed into general waste.

name: Gear Pump
description: A positive displacement pump that uses meshing gears to move hydraulic fluid with consistent pressure and flow, commonly used in lubrication systems.
whyMatters: Energy — preserves tight machining tolerances and a complete fluid-power device, not just raw metal.

name: High Pressure Gas Spring
description: A gas-filled cylinder that provides controlled force and damping, often used in blade pitch systems or mechanical braking.
whyMatters: Materials — keeps a pressure-rated steel cylinder and polished rod in use where safe requalification is possible.

name: High tensil bolts
description: Extremely strong fasteners made from high-strength steel alloys, capable of withstanding enormous forces in critical structural connections.
whyMatters: Materials — retains high-strength alloy steel and the energy-intensive heat treatment embedded in critical fasteners.

name: Inductive Proximity Switch
description: A non-contact sensor that detects metal objects nearby using electromagnetic fields, used for position sensing and safety monitoring.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — keeps small sensor electronics, copper coils and ferrite material out of e-waste.

name: Inductive Sensor
description: A sensor that detects metal objects without physical contact, commonly used for position monitoring and safety systems.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — avoids disposal of a small but material-diverse electronics device.

name: Interface Relay
description: An electrical switch that isolates and controls circuits, allowing low-voltage control signals to safely operate high-power equipment.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — recovers or reuses contact metals, copper coil and plastic enclosure instead of creating mixed e-waste.

name: Main I-Beam
description: A large structural steel beam with an I-shaped cross-section, providing crucial structural support in the turbine tower or nacelle frame.
whyMatters: Materials — keeps a large load-bearing metal member in structural service, which is usually higher value than melting it immediately.

name: Main Switchgear
description: The primary electrical distribution and protection equipment that routes power from the generator and protects against electrical faults.
whyMatters: Mining pressure — keeps conductor metals and steel enclosures in circulation, while avoiding new grid-equipment material demand.

name: Non-Fusible Disconnect Switch
description: A heavy-duty manual switch for safely disconnecting electrical power, used for maintenance and emergency shutdown.
whyMatters: Materials — preserves durable switching hardware and conductor metals when tested and reused safely.

name: Nose Cone
description: The aerodynamic cover at the center of the rotor hub where the blades meet, reducing drag and protecting internal components.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — keeps a bulky composite shell from becoming hard-to-recycle disposal burden.

name: Power Supply
description: An electronic device that converts and regulates electrical power to the correct voltage and current for control systems and sensors.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — keeps e-waste out of disposal while recovering copper, aluminium and circuit-board materials.

name: Powercable
description: Heavy-duty electrical cable designed to carry high currents safely, with appropriate insulation and protection for industrial environments.
whyMatters: Mining pressure — conductor metals are the major value, especially copper and aluminium, and reuse/recycling reduces virgin extraction.

name: Pressure Switch
description: A sensor that monitors fluid or air pressure and triggers actions when pressure reaches specified thresholds, used for safety and control.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — keeps a mixed metal-and-electrical instrument from becoming small unmanaged industrial waste.

name: Resistor
description: An electronic component that limits electrical current flow, used in circuits for voltage division, current limiting, and signal conditioning.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — small components add up; controlled recovery avoids scattering ceramics, metals and polymers through mixed e-waste.

name: Safety Relay
description: A specialized relay designed for safety-critical applications, with redundant contacts and self-monitoring to ensure reliable operation.
whyMatters: Public value — tested units can support safety training rigs, spares programs or educational automation panels while avoiding e-waste.

name: Service Lift
description: An elevator or lifting platform inside the turbine tower that transports personnel and equipment between the base and nacelle.
whyMatters: Public value — can become access, training, maintenance or warehouse utility if certified for the new use case.

name: Spacer
description: A mechanical component used to maintain precise gaps between parts, ensuring proper alignment and preventing unwanted contact.
whyMatters: Materials — preserves small precision-machined material and avoids remaking simple but tolerance-critical parts.

name: Surge Arrester
description: A protective device that diverts lightning strikes and electrical surges to ground, protecting sensitive electrical equipment.
whyMatters: Waste avoided — prevents ceramic, metal and polymer protection devices from becoming mixed electrical waste.

name: Switchgear
description: Electrical switching and protection equipment that controls, protects, and isolates electrical circuits in the power distribution system.
whyMatters: Mining pressure — high-current conductor metals and steel cabinets carry the greatest environmental value.

name: Switching Power Gear
description: High-voltage switching equipment that routes electrical power between the generator, transformer, and grid connection.
whyMatters: Mining pressure — avoids new copper/aluminium/steel demand in grid-scale switching equipment.

name: Torque Shaft
description: A heavy-duty rotating shaft designed to transmit high torque forces between the rotor hub and gearbox or generator.
whyMatters: Materials — preserves a massive forged alloy-steel component and the energy-intensive machining/heat-treatment already embedded in it.

name: Transformer
description: An electrical device that changes voltage levels, stepping up turbine-generated power to grid voltage or stepping down power for internal systems.
whyMatters: Mining pressure — keeps copper, aluminium and electrical steel in circulation, all important for grid expansion and electrification.

name: Ultrasone Wind Sensors
description: Advanced wind sensors that use ultrasonic sound waves to precisely measure wind speed and direction without moving parts.
whyMatters: Public value — can be reused for weather stations, schools, farm monitoring or local climate data projects.

name: Vickers Pressure Control Valve
description: A hydraulic control valve that regulates fluid pressure in hydraulic systems, maintaining optimal operating conditions.
whyMatters: Energy — preserves precision hydraulic machining and a durable control function, not just the scrap value of the metal.

name: Wind Vane
description: A traditional directional sensor that shows which way the wind is blowing, helping the turbine optimize its orientation.
whyMatters: Public value — can become a community weather, education or farm-orientation tool with low refurbishment effort.
